author: nflaig
content: I would like to question that decision and re-ask it, it seems like a stretch to me to add this whole pre-registration **again** if we also sent it JIT just becasue 1 builder iirc said they need it to seal the block 0.01 sec faster or something, I don't understand how that complexity is justified. We can discuss if this pre-registration of builder preferences (few slots, or even an epoch) earlier is useful in general, there might be more in the future I don't know that, but at this moment, it's seems to help builders on a at best micro optimization, this `max_exec_payment` is kinda absurd in the first place, how do you even decide the value of this, I mean sure it's useful for `max_exec_payment=0` (no trusted bids) and `max_exec_payment=2^64 - 1` (trusted bids accepted), but other than that it's questionable at best, like the above `1ETH a builder may want to send them 1ETH trusted and 0.5ETH trustless`, if this happens once in the year after gloas is on mainnet, I owe you a beer

author: potuz
content: Cc <@&1458826346896687229> , I'm just too hung over to find the link to the conversation

author: nflaig
content: around [here](https://t.me/c/3709773243/148), I don't think this was ever discussed in public, just this private telegram group. but besides the point, I don't really mind adding the pre-registration to much, I think the design we have is to only do it for upcoming proposer right? and not the madness we have currently that sends all validators via register_validator each epoch, this was needed pre-fulu, but shouldn't be relevant with the stable lookahead. What I do mind though is making protocol decisions based on few comments in a private telegram group that apparently not even the author of epbs is able to find (just kidding potuz), so at least let's have this on a more public record, and also let others join the discussion
